author: brechy
content:
Regarding this, there's no specific reason. I would perhaps hold off from modifying the `head` check, since I understand some clients only check `head` on FC tests (cc <@755590043632140352> ) and this would introduce breaking changes on current pipelines. 

Do you think there's a good call to take this route instead?

author: m.kalinin
content: Even though I believe that `head.payload_status` in the test format is more natural and convenient, I admit that it might be too late to change that because i believe there are clients that already run Gloas tests with `head_payload_status`

author: potuz
content: The condition of only checking head is meant to be that spectests should not test for internal structures. But the output of head is precisely what forkchoice does, so it should be tested. After Gloas the head payload status is part of the head so it is very important that we do test it

author: brechy
content:
Ah, thanks for the clarification, I understood it as only checking correct root, so that only the actual head selection is tested. 

A PR updating this check could then be justified in line of keeping the same data structure defined in the specs, as:

```python
class ForkChoiceNode(Container):
    root: Root
    payload_status: PayloadStatus
```

<@425572898787426305> Do you think this is too big of a change for clients that already have it implemented?

author: m.kalinin
content: idk, might not be too big. we can ask client teams about that change on next ACDT

author: potuz
content: I think using this container would be fine for us. cc <@363800010518822915> just in case
